events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`: a sling angle under 30° from horizontal is a **fail** (high risk). At that angle each leg's tension roughly doubles versus a vertical hitch, pushing the rigging toward or past its rated working load limit — a well-known hazard under ASME B30.9 sling angle/capacity guidance and OSHA 1926.251(c). Recommended action is to stop the lift and re-rig to at least 30° (45°+ preferred) before resuming.
